content:
2 min read
Just now

--

There are thousands of websites where you can learn programming.
So why did I spend months building another one?
Because I wanted a platform that I would actually enjoy using.
Whenever I started learning a new topic, I found myself jumping between countless tabs — blogs for concepts, another site for interview questions, documentation, developer tools, YouTube videos… everything was scattered.
It worked, but it never felt like one place built for developers.
So I decided to build **CodeCareer4U**.
Not because the internet needed another coding website.
But I wanted a platform that brought everything I personally needed into one place.
Over the past few months, I’ve been working on it whenever I had free time — writing articles, building developer tools, fixing bugs, redesigning pages, and constantly improving the experience.
Today, CodeCareer4U includes:
📚 Easy-to-understand technical articles
💼 Thousands of interview questions to help developers prepare with confidence
🛠️ Free developer tools that I use in my own projects
💻 Learning resources for React, Next.js, JavaScript, TypeScript, Node.js, AI, Cloud, and more
The best part?
I’m not trying to “finish” this platform.
I want it to keep evolving based on what developers actually need.
If you’re a student, a software engineer, or someone preparing for interviews, I’d really appreciate it if you could spend just a few minutes exploring it.
🌐 https://codecareer4u.com
And if you do, I’d love to hear one thing:
**What’s one feature or improvement that would make you come back and use it regularly?**
I’m reading every comment, and many of the upcoming features will be built from your feedback.
Thank you to everyone who’s supported this journey so far. There’s still a long way to go, but I’m excited to keep building. ❤️
#WebDevelopment #ReactJS #NextJS #JavaScript #TypeScript #NodeJS #SoftwareEngineering #Coding #DeveloperTools #BuildInPublic
